query: migration of plasticisers from plastic food contact materials

t1.1.name: Temperature and Time Dependencies
t1.1.description: Investigation of how storage temperature, heating conditions, and contact duration influence plasticiser migration rates from food contact materials.

t1.2.name: Food Simulant and Matrix Effects
t1.2.description: How different food types (fatty, aqueous, acidic) and standardized simulants affect migration behavior based on chemical compatibility and lipophilicity.

t1.name: Plasticiser Migration Mechanisms and Factors
t1.description: Core scientific understanding of how plasticisers migrate from packaging into food, including diffusion processes, partition coefficients, and environmental/material factors affecting migration rates.

t2.description: Technical approaches and instrumentation used to measure and quantify plasticiser migration, including extraction protocols, chromatographic techniques, and mass spectrometry methods.

t3.name: Health Risk Assessment and Exposure
t3.description: Evaluation of human exposure levels to migrated plasticisers through diet, toxicological endpoints, and regulatory safety thresholds for food contact applications.

t1.2.description: Examination of substitute plasticizers including adipates, citrates, trimellitates, and bio-based alternatives developed to replace phthalates in food contact applications.

t1.description: Overview of chemical structures, classifications, and properties of plasticizers commonly used in food contact materials, including phthalates, adipates, and alternative plasticizers.

t2.1.description: How food characteristics such as fat content, pH, alcohol content, and temperature affect plasticizer migration rates and extent of transfer from packaging materials.

t2.name: Migration Mechanisms and Kinetics
t2.description: Fundamental processes governing plasticizer migration from polymer matrices into food, including diffusion theory, partition coefficients, and factors affecting migration rates such as temperature, contact time, and food composition.

t4.description: International and regional regulations governing plasticizer use in food contact materials, including specific migration limits (SML), overall migration limits (OML), and tolerable daily intake (TDI) values established by regulatory agencies.
